serviceName: TaxEZ A. Core Individual Tax Return (1040)
Internal Sales Sheet:
# Internal Service Guide: TaxEZ A. Core Individual Tax Return (1040)

## 1. Service Overview
This service involves preparing and filing the Core Individual Tax Return (1040) for clients. It addresses the common challenges individuals face during tax season, such as understanding tax obligations, maximizing deductions, and ensuring compliance with IRS regulations. By providing this service, we help clients navigate their tax responsibilities efficiently and accurately.

## 2. Step-by-Step Delivery Process
1. Client Intake: Collect necessary information from the client, including income details, deductions, and any relevant tax documents.
2. Review Documents: Verify all submitted documents for completeness and accuracy.
3. Prepare Tax Return: Utilize the appropriate software to fill out the 1040 form based on the client’s information.
4. Review & Quality Check: Conduct a thorough review of the completed tax return for accuracy and compliance.
5. Client Approval: Send the draft return to the client for review and approval.
6. File Tax Return: Submit the finalized 1040 form electronically to the IRS.
7. Post-Filing Follow-Up: Confirm receipt of the tax return and answer any client questions regarding next steps.

## 3. Required Tools & Resources
- Tax Preparation Software: Use TaxEZ or similar approved platforms.
- IRS Guidelines: Reference the latest IRS publications to ensure compliance.
- Internal Templates: Utilize checklists and templates for document collection and quality checks.

## 4. Pro Tips & Insights
- Maintain Clear Communication: Regularly update clients on the status of their tax return and any additional information needed.
- Double-Check Entries: Always verify entered data against original documents to avoid errors.
- Stay Updated: Be aware of any recent tax law changes that may impact the client's return.
- Educate Clients: Provide information on potential deductions and credits relevant to their situation.
- Time Management: Allow enough time for review and client feedback to avoid last-minute rushes.

## 5. Quality Standards / Final Deliverables
- ✅ Completed 1040 tax return filed with the IRS.
- ✅ Confirmation of e-filing receipt from the IRS.
- ✅ A summary report of the tax return provided to the client.
- ✅ Clear documentation of any deductions and credits applied.

## 6. Time Estimate
🕒 3–5 hours on average per tax return (includes intake, preparation, review, and filing).

## 7. Escalation Points
🚩 If the client’s documents are incomplete or unclear, escalate for further clarification or additional document requests.

🚩 If any discrepancies arise during the preparation, seek input from a senior tax advisor.

🚩 If client questions exceed standard scope, notify the client of additional consultation fees.
Required Documents:
To start or complete this service, the following documents, access, or information are needed from the client:

- Previous year's tax returns
- W-2 forms from employers
- 1099 forms for any freelance or contract work
- Records of any other income (e.g., rental income, interest income)
- Documentation of deductible expenses (e.g., receipts for business expenses, medical expenses)
- Information about any dependents
- Social Security numbers for all individuals on the return
- Bank account information for direct deposit of refunds
deliverables: At the end of this service, the client will receive a completed Core Individual Tax Return (Form 1040) that accurately reflects their financial information for the tax year. This includes all necessary calculations, deductions, and credits applied to optimize their tax return. Additionally, the client will receive a summary of their tax situation, outlining any potential refunds or amounts owed, along with guidance on any next steps required for filing.
exclusions:
This service does not include:

- Tax planning strategies for future years
- Representation during IRS audits or disputes
- Advanced tax scenarios such as self-employment income, rental properties, or investments
- State tax return preparation
- Refund anticipation loans
- Consulting on tax-related legal matters
- Custom financial advice tailored to individual circumstances

- Document Everything: Keep detailed notes of discrepancies and communications for future reference.
- Set a Timeline: Establish a timeline for the review process to manage client expectations effectively.

# 🔧 Internal Service Guide: Digital - Drive System & Permissions (Standard)

## 1. Service Overview
This service involves creating a structured, user-friendly digital drive system with appropriate permission controls. It addresses the needs of organizations seeking to streamline document management, improve collaboration, and enhance security through defined access levels.

## 2. Step-by-Step Delivery Process
1. Client Intake: Initiate a conversation with the client to understand their organizational structure and specific needs.
2. Tool Selection: Determine the platform to be used (e.g., Google Drive, OneDrive).
3. Structure Drafting: Design a folder hierarchy that caters to the client's departments and projects.
4. Permission Mapping: Establish access levels (View, Edit, Admin) for each folder based on user roles.
5. Setup & Sync: Implement the folder structure and permissions on the selected platform.
6. Test Access: Conduct tests using a test user account to ensure permissions are correctly set.
7. Client Walkthrough: Schedule a walkthrough session to guide the client through the new system.

## 3. Required Tools & Resources
- Platform access (Google Drive, OneDrive, etc.)
- Internal permission mapping template
- SOPs for naming conventions
- Optional: Tools for screen sharing (Zoom, Loom)

## 4. Pro Tips & Insights
- Always confirm admin access before starting the setup.
- Maintain consistent naming conventions for clarity.
- Limit folder nesting to three levels to avoid confusion.
- Save a backup of the structure plan in a shared document.
- Avoid granting excessive editing permissions to prevent chaos in document management.

## 5. Quality Standards / Final Deliverables
- ✅ Implemented and verified folder structure
- ✅ Mapped and tested permissions
- ✅ Access summary document provided to the client
- ✅ Recorded walkthrough session shared with the client
- ✅ Documentation of sensitive access in the client’s CRM record

## 6. Time Estimate
🕒 Average setup time is 2–3 hours:
- 1 hour for initial consultation and mapping
- 1–2 hours for setup and testing

## 7. Escalation Points
🚩 If the client lacks a clear organizational structure, escalate for a strategy consultation.
🚩 If access issues arise due to platform limitations, contact technical support.
🚩 If the project scope increases to include automation, flag for an upgrade to the Advanced Drive System service.
Required Documents:
To start or complete this service, the following documents, access, or information are needed from the client:

- Client's account details for income tracking
- Access to existing cloud services documentation
- Any specific requirements or preferences for the digital drive system
- Confirmation of user permissions and roles within the system
- Information on any integration with existing tools or software
